channel.title: Cariboo Signals
channel.copyright: © 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.
channel.itunes:subtitle: Technology and society in rural BC with Riley and Casey
channel.itunes:author: Erich Zirnhelt
channel.itunes:summary:
How do rural communities grow alongside technology? Daily conversations about responsible tech progress in the Cariboo region on the traditional territories of the Secwépemc, Tŝilhqot'in, and Dakelh nations. Riley brings tech systems thinking with rural roots, while Casey focuses on community development. New episodes every day with weekly themes.
Community Accountability: Cariboo Signals scores 18/20 (Exemplary) on the Community Content Compact — Track A (Community-Insider production). Transparency: 4/4 · Source Integrity: 3/3 · Displacement: 2/2 · Consent & Attribution: 3/4 · Benefit Flow: 4/5 · Accountability: 2/2. Produced by Erich Zirnhelt, Horsefly, BC (Secwépemc territory). Corrections and PSA opt-outs: feedback@cariboosignals.ca.
channel.description: How do rural communities grow alongside technology? Daily conversations about responsible tech progress in the Cariboo region on the traditional territories of the Secwépemc, Tŝilhqot'in, and Dakelh nations. Community Content Compact: 18/20 Exemplary (Track A). Self-funded, non-commercial. Producer: Erich Zirnhelt, Horsefly, BC. Corrections: feedback@cariboosignals.ca.

trace:assessment.trace:community: Rural Cariboo region, BC — Secwépemc, Tŝilhqot'in, and Dakelh territories

item.title: Wild Spaces And Outdoor Life
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Wild Spaces & Outdoor Life.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Al Jazeera English] Bolivian farmers clash with police as ex-president vows resistance and [Boing Boing] In 1932, John W. Campbell imagined machines outliving humanity, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How can wildlife management frameworks keep pace with changing wild spaces, particularly regarding grizzly population recovery and human encroachment in the Chilcotin? Topics include: Yellowstone camping ban and grizzly population recovery as management case study, Jurisdictional gaps between provincial wildlife management and Tŝilhqot'in Aboriginal title in the Chilcotin, Remote monitoring technology capabilities and cost-benefit analysis, Sustainability challenges of grant-dependent monitoring infrastructure, Pre-trip stewardship communication protocols as governance model.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Cariboo Family Enrichment Centre

Sources discussed:

  • Al Jazeera English: [Al Jazeera English] Bolivian farmers clash with police as e...
  • Ellsworth Toohey (Boing Boing): [Boing Boing] In 1932, John W. Campbell imagined machines ou...
  • Anna Washenko (Engadget): [Engadget] Another parent has filed a wrongful death suit ag...
  • Associated Press (STAT News): [STAT News] FDA OKs first new sunscreen ingredient in more t...
  • none (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] Xat’sull First Nation bringing Calli...

Additional sources provided:

  • none (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] B.C. man fined $6,000 for illegally ...
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] After seeing Parkinson's take his father,...
  • Mohamed Hussein (Al Jazeera English): [Al Jazeera English] Iran’s lakes are vanishing: Satellite i...
  • Madison Dapcevich (Outside Online): [Outside Online] Officials Have Officially Banned Tents at a...
  • https://www.techradar.com/author/craig-hale (TechRadar): [TechRadar] Nashville Zoo leads fight against mega AI data c...

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
Script Review Model: claude-sonnet-4-6
TTS Voices: OpenAI TTS API (Nova · Echo)
Web Search: Brave Search API
Weather Data: Open-Meteo
Cover Art: ChatGPT (OpenAI)
Podcast Coordination: Erich Zirnhelt
© 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How can wildlife management frameworks keep pace with changing wild spaces, particularly regarding grizzly population recovery and human encroachment in the Chilcotin? Topics include: Yellowstone camping ban and grizzly population recovery as management case study, Jurisdictional gaps between provincial wildlife management and Tŝilhqot'in Aboriginal title in the Chilcotin, Remote monitoring technology capabilities and cost-benefit analysis, Sustainability challenges of grant-dependent monitoring infrastructure, Pre-trip stewardship communication protocols as governance model.

item.title: Indigenous Lands And Innovation
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Indigenous Lands & Innovation.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [BC Gov News] Opening new homes for Indigenous families in Prince Rupert and [Engadget] German court holds Google liable for false AI Overview answers, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Should Indigenous knowledge systems be integrated into existing technology and data infrastructure, or should the conceptual framework itself be fundamentally reimagined starting from Indigenous land-based governance? Topics include: Integration vs. foundational reimagining of Indigenous knowledge systems in technology, Data sovereignty, OCAP principles, and the limits of encoding relational knowledge, Institutional independence and funding relationships as constraints on accountability, Community-led infrastructure ownership versus service delivery models, Specific case studies: Animikii, FirstVoices, Tŝilhqot'in, Lhtako Dené Nation, and BCFNJC Police Accountability Unit.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Denisiqi Services Society

Sources discussed:

  • BC Gov News: [BC Gov News] Opening new homes for Indigenous families in P...
  • Lawrence Bonk (Engadget): [Engadget] German court holds Google liable for false AI Ove...
  • Newsletter: n***@animikii.com: Robots behaving badly VS what the land knows

Additional sources provided:

  • none (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] Clearwater mayor speaks at national ...
  • Jason Weisberger (Boing Boing): [Boing Boing] Nancy Pelosi has no time for Mike Lindell's ha...
  • Gaby Del Valle (The Verge): [The Verge] Congress just gave DHS another $70 billion
  • rebeccadyok (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] Rotary Peace Pole to be installed in...
  • BC Gov News: [BC Gov News] Sharing local farm harvests through coupon pro...
  • https://www.tomsguide.com/author/ruth-jones (Toms Guide): [Toms Guide] Anxiety and stress were keeping me awake — unti...
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] I gave up my career at a Fortune 500 comp...
  • Chloe Gerrard (Newsletter: n***@animikii.com): Genome Canada, Genome Prairie Launch $50M National Resources...
  • Jordy Cunningham (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] Indigenous policing accountability p...

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
Script Review Model: claude-sonnet-4-6
TTS Voices: OpenAI TTS API (Nova · Echo)
Web Search: Brave Search API
Cover Art: ChatGPT (OpenAI)
Podcast Coordination: Erich Zirnhelt
© 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.

item.title: Gear, Gadgets And Practical Tech
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Gear, Gadgets & Practical Tech.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Al Jazeera English] South Korean police break up 35-hour polling station protest and [Al Jazeera English] Ebola border shutdown leaves goods rotting between Uganda and DRC, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How can rural communities like the Cariboo access reliable information about which gear and gadgets actually perform durably in remote, cold conditions without manufacturer support or institutional resources? Topics include: Battery chemistry performance in cold climates and manufacturer lock-in practices, The gap between institutional equipment specifications (SAR, wildfire) and what individual rural users can access, Formal vs. informal knowledge infrastructure and the discoverability problem, Structural sustainability of community databases and the handoff from student projects to permanent hosting, Municipal infrastructure as a persistence layer for community knowledge versus grant-dependent websites.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Cedar Crest Society for Community Living

Sources discussed:

  • Al Jazeera English: [Al Jazeera English] South Korean police break up 35-hour po...
  • Al Jazeera English: [Al Jazeera English] Ebola border shutdown leaves goods rott...
  • Al Jazeera English: [Al Jazeera English] Bolivia approves military measures agai...
  • Al Jazeera English: [Al Jazeera English] Sherpa found alive on Mount Everest aft...
  • Al Jazeera English: [Al Jazeera English] Huge fire erupts at Honda warehouse in ...
  • Al Jazeera English: [Al Jazeera English] Magnitude 7.8 earthquake rocks southern...
  • Cheyenne MacDonald (Engadget): [Engadget] Astronomers measure the mass of a dormant black h...

  • Al Jazeera English: [Al Jazeera English] Israeli forces kill nine in Tyre after ...

  • Al Jazeera English: [Al Jazeera English] Gaza fishermen stay afloat with dinghie...
  • Al Jazeera English: [Al Jazeera English] London activists try to erect statue of...

item.title: Working Lands And Industry
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Working Lands & Industry.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Business Insider] A consulting firm is now using AI to give free advice that once cost millions and [CBC Arts] Hasbro releases AI versions of its iconic characters, including Transformers, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Who captures the economic value generated by resource extraction and infrastructure development in the Cariboo-Chilcotin region, and how can communities ensure they retain benefits rather than bearing costs? Topics include: Carbon markets as mechanisms for pricing ecosystem services and their volatility risks, Ownership structure differences between Indigenous joint ventures and external contractor models, Data centre infrastructure costs to watersheds and power systems versus concentrated financial benefits, Asset ownership versus management contracts as pathways to permanent value capture, Technical and institutional capacity constraints preventing communities from negotiating favorable terms.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Central Chilcotin Rehabilitation Ltd.

Sources discussed:

  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] A consulting firm is now using AI to give...
  • CBC Arts: [CBC Arts] Hasbro releases AI versions of its iconic charact...

  • Yashvika Grover (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] Bella Coola residents raise environm...
  • Laisa Conde (Quesnel Cariboo Observer): [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] B.C. Air Access Program earmarks ...
  • none (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] B.C. doctors ratify new 4-year deal,...

  • https://www.techradar.com/author/craig-hale (TechRadar): [TechRadar] 'Some places carry the costs and other places ca...
  • Yashvika Grover (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] B.C. funds 16 forest resiliency proj...

Additional sources provided:

  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] Read the pitch deck startup Unframe used ...
  • Jackson Chen (Engadget): [Engadget] Nintendo will pay a $40 million fine for faulty J...
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] An ex-lawyer has raised $70 million from ...
  • The Narwhal: [The Narwhal] Despite soaring gold prices, the Yukon can’t c...
  • Al Jazeera Staff (Al Jazeera English): [Al Jazeera English] EU planning to add to $1.5 trillion san...
  • https://newatlas.com/author/monica-j-white/ (New Atlas): [New Atlas] Dual-screen monitor can show two sides of the sa...
  • https://newatlas.com/author/utkarsh-sood/ (New Atlas): [New Atlas] Guess which bike hides beneath this 3.9-meter-lo...
  • none (100 Mile Free Press): [100 Mile Free Press] HANSEN: Flowers, summer events in bloo...
  • Al Jazeera Staff (Al Jazeera English): [Al Jazeera English] China’s Xi, North Korea’s Kim pledge to...

item.title: Arts, Culture And Digital Storytelling
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Arts, Culture & Digital Storytelling.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Business Insider] Music festivals gave me hearing loss. My best friend and I started making earplugs, and it's now a multimillion-dollar business. and [Al Jazeera English] Kenyan graduates turn to AI tools for farming as jobs dry up, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: When platforms choose not to filter or label AI-generated content despite having the technical capability, who bears the cost and how can authentic regional cultural production survive? Topics include: Platform design choices around AI content filtering and their impact on authentic regional cultural visibility, Capital asymmetry between AI companies and regional arts organizations, Discovery algorithms as a mechanism that makes synthetic content more visible than authentic local storytelling, Cooperative digital infrastructure models as alternative to platform-dependent distribution, Indigenous cultural production and platform-independent knowledge systems as structural resistance to AI generation.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Station House Gallery

Sources discussed:

  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] Music festivals gave me hearing loss. My ...
  • Dominic Kirui (Al Jazeera English): [Al Jazeera English] Kenyan graduates turn to AI tools for f...
  • Jess Weatherbed (The Verge): [The Verge] Let us filter AI slop, you cowards
  • Amanda Silberling (TechCrunch): [TechCrunch] Still facing copyright lawsuits, AI music gener...
  • https://www.theguardian.com/profile/kenneth-mohammed (The Guardian Global Development): [The Guardian Global Development] It’s no surprise that an A...
  • https://www.tomsguide.com/author/amanda-caswell (Toms Guide): [Toms Guide] Comedian Ronny Chieng says 'AI is just gonna en...
  • none (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] One fatality in vehicle crash on Hig...

Additional sources provided:

  • Newsletter: c***@openmedia.org: Stop the Surveillance State: Stop Bill C-22!
  • Newsletter: s***@canadaland.com: #1374 Introducing the Nuance: Chrystia Freeland on Trump and...
  • Newsletter: s***@canadaland.com: #184 Carney Wants Your Data. Why Won't Big Tech Give it...
  • Newsletter: s***@canadaland.com: Discover Our Cell Phone Plans | Fizz
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] I moved from the UK to the US to grow my ...
  • NPR Health News: [NPR Health News] CDC report: Ebola outbreak could rival the...
  • Ram Iyer (TechCrunch): [TechCrunch] GitLab cuts 14% of staff as it scales its platf...
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] I use AI at home because I'm a working mo...

  • none (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] BRIONES: Going cuckoo over photograp...

Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno

item.itunes:summary:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Arts, Culture & Digital Storytelling.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Business Insider] Music festivals gave me hearing loss. My best friend and I started making earplugs, and it's now a multimillion-dollar business. and [Al Jazeera English] Kenyan graduates turn to AI tools for farming as jobs dry up, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: When platforms choose not to filter or label AI-generated content despite having the technical capability, who bears the cost and how can authentic regional cultural production survive? Topics include: Platform design choices around AI content filtering and their impact on authentic regional cultural visibility, Capital asymmetry between AI companies and regional arts organizations, Discovery algorithms as a mechanism that makes synthetic content more visible than authentic local storytelling, Cooperative digital infrastructure models as alternative to platform-dependent distribution, Indigenous cultural production and platform-independent knowledge systems as structural resistance to AI generation.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Station House Gallery

Sources discussed:

  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] Music festivals gave me hearing loss. My ...
  • Dominic Kirui (Al Jazeera English): [Al Jazeera English] Kenyan graduates turn to AI tools for f...
  • Jess Weatherbed (The Verge): [The Verge] Let us filter AI slop, you cowards
  • Amanda Silberling (TechCrunch): [TechCrunch] Still facing copyright lawsuits, AI music gener...
  • https://www.theguardian.com/profile/kenneth-mohammed (The Guardian Global Development): [The Guardian Global Development] It’s no surprise that an A...
  • https://www.tomsguide.com/author/amanda-caswell (Toms Guide): [Toms Guide] Comedian Ronny Chieng says 'AI is just gonna en...
  • none (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] One fatality in vehicle crash on Hig...

Additional sources provided:

  • Newsletter: c***@openmedia.org: Stop the Surveillance State: Stop Bill C-22!
  • Newsletter: s***@canadaland.com: #1374 Introducing the Nuance: Chrystia Freeland on Trump and...
  • Newsletter: s***@canadaland.com: #184 Carney Wants Your Data. Why Won't Big Tech Give it...
  • Newsletter: s***@canadaland.com: Discover Our Cell Phone Plans | Fizz
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] I moved from the UK to the US to grow my ...
  • NPR Health News: [NPR Health News] CDC report: Ebola outbreak could rival the...
  • Ram Iyer (TechCrunch): [TechCrunch] GitLab cuts 14% of staff as it scales its platf...
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] I use AI at home because I'm a working mo...
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] The AI trade is having a terrible day
  • none (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] Bodies of 2 paddleboarders recovered...
  • none (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] BRIONES: Going cuckoo over photograp...
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] AI spending from 4 tech giants will excee...
  • Newsletter: s***@canadaland.com: #1370 Ebola: 28 Years Later
  • Newsletter: s***@canadaland.com: #183 Is Carney Chinamaxxing?

item.title: Science, Wonder And The Natural World
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Science, Wonder & the Natural World.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Business Insider] The AI industry's profit problem is getting bigger, says Goldman Sachs' equity research chief and [Business Insider] I flew on a startup's one-of-a-kind electric plane that could reshape air travel by 2027, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Can AI tools reshape ecological science in the Cariboo in ways that serve community interests, or do they primarily amplify existing extractive research structures? Topics include: Infrastructure costs of AI and ecological impact of data centres, Indigenous data sovereignty and structural exclusion from peer-reviewed research systems, Edge-deployed vs. cloud-based AI models for environmental monitoring, Community-owned data licensing and fee-for-access ecological knowledge systems, Funding and sustainability models for long-term community AI governance infrastructure.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Community Futures Cariboo Chilcotin

Sources discussed:

  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] The AI industry's profit problem is getti...
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] I flew on a startup's one-of-a-kind elect...
  • Maria Popova (The Marginalian): [The Marginalian] Ursula K. Le Guin on the Meaning of Life
  • Laisa Conde (Quesnel Cariboo Observer): [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] Prince George RCMP confirm 3 rece...
  • The Narwhal: [The Narwhal] In New Brunswick, residents battle the governm...

Additional sources provided:

  • https://www.tomsguide.com/author/alyse-stanley (Toms Guide): [Toms Guide] Netflix just added a batch of feel-good movies ...
  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] The forgotten organ that could predict how lo...
  • https://www.tomsguide.com/author/nikita-achanta (Toms Guide): [Toms Guide] I ditched my JBL Go 5 for the $49 8BitDo Retro ...
  • The Conversation (ScienceAlert): [ScienceAlert] AI Could Soon Use More Water Than Humanity Dr...
  • Al Jazeera English: [Al Jazeera English] ‘Elections campaigned on who can be mor...
  • Al Jazeera Staff (Al Jazeera English): [Al Jazeera English] ‘Pervasive fear’ grips Gaza as Israeli ...
  • https://www.techradar.com/author/lance-ulanoff (TechRadar): [TechRadar] I just watched a robot kick a child and I'm over...
  • Words and photographs by Pelumi Salako in Yawota (The Guardian Global Development): [The Guardian Global Development] Gunshots at 9am. Then they...
  • Al Jazeera English: [Al Jazeera English] US says Iranian radar sites hit in Goru...
  • jenzielinski (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] Locked out Kelowna daycare files sui...
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] Some job seekers are running out of money...
  • Al Jazeera English: [Al Jazeera English] Kyiv residents seek shelter during rene...
  • Android Authority: [Android Authority] Tired of AI making stuff up? This assist...
  • https://www.tomsguide.com/author/amanda-caswell (Toms Guide): [Toms Guide] I tested local AI vs. ChatGPT side-by-side — he...

Closing Music: “So Called Democracy” by Stephane TV — rock, indie, indierock. Free music via Jamendo, licensed under Creative Commons.

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
Script Review Model: claude-sonnet-4-6
TTS Voices: OpenAI TTS API (Nova · Echo)
Web Search: Brave Search API
Weather Data: Open-Meteo
Cover Art: ChatGPT (OpenAI)
Podcast Coordination: Erich Zirnhelt
© 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Business Insider] The AI industry's profit problem is getting bigger, says Goldman Sachs' equity research chief and [Business Insider] I flew on a startup's one-of-a-kind electric plane that could reshape air travel by 2027, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.
